records.ety: [L. expiratio,exspiratio: cf. F. expiration. See Expire.]

defs: The act or process of breathing out, or forcing air from the lungs through the nose or mouth; as, respiration consists of inspiration and expiration; -- opposed to inspiration.
The last emission of breath; death.
A coming to a close; cessation; extinction; termination; end.
quotes: The true cause of cold is an expiration from the globe of the earth.
Before the expiration of thy time.

defs: That which is expired; matter breathed forth; that which is produced by breathing out, as a sound.
quotes: The aspirate “he,” which is . . . a gentle expiration.
